Scripts: Module 2
SCRIPTS
TO TELL THE STORY
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
My name is John Smith. In the next 20 minutes you’re going to here about
my company, Chelexa. You’re going to see some remarkable products we’ve
built. And how life is pretty damn good for us now that we have these great
products.
But it wasn’t always like this.
Several years ago, when I started, I was told it couldn’t be done. My own
brother, a venture capitalist, told me not to invest my money. My friend who
ran a large hospital told me that even I created the product, he wouldn’t
buy it. But I knew this product could be created. From each iteration, each
new attempt to build the product, I learned something new. We didn’t have
enough money along the way, for a full lab, so we rented a lab and did an
enormous amount of computer modeling (which is more expensive but ruins
your weekends.) And last year, I had a breakthrough. It was one of the first
times I realized, being stubborn can pay off. Risking your own money can
pay off. Following the science and trusting your engineers, can truly create a
breakthrough...
The Key To a Good
Performance
Be expressive. Facial animation, eye
movements,, and gestures I language,
and energy level can all tell a narrative as
powerfully as the words themselves.
Experiment with accentuating different
thoughts and emotions with specific
movements. Try changing your gestures
her tone of voice when you’re quoting
other people. And feel free to use any
props within arms reach—a cell phone, a
straw.
However, be careful not to overdo it. The
smaller and more subtle your gestures
and affectations are, the more credible
they’ll be.
Build a Compelling Character I
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
My name is John Smith. In the next 20 minutes you’re going to here about
my firm, Acme. You’re going to hear some very interesting insights into the
market we operate in, but before I do that let me tell you how I got here.
I was not born into the halls of Acme. I grew up in the Depression in
Brooklyn’s Borough Park neighborhood. My parents were immigrants from
Eastern Europe. They were desperately poor, and we moved nearly every
year because the custom in those days was for landlords to give new tenants
a month’s free rent, and without that, we could not get by. In junior high
school, I took the entrance exam for Townsend Harris public high school on
Lexington Avenue in Manhattan, a school that in just forty years of existence
produced three Nobel Prize winners, six Pulitzer Prize winners, and one
Supreme Court Justice, not to mention George Gershwin and Jonas Salk, the
inventor of the polio vaccine.
Well. I got in.
My mother would give me a dime in the morning for breakfast—three
donuts, orange juice, and coffee at Nedick’s. After school, I pushed a hand
truck in the garment district. I did two years of night school at City College in
Build a Compelling Character II Five Minutes to Showtime
Relax. In the room, you get bombarded
with stress. If there are other executives
or business people nearby they will
want to small talk. Administrative staff
will bump into you. All of your fears will
escalate as each minute passes.
Chances are you won’t have an office
to yourself, so find some quiet corner
where you can be alone and connect with
yourself. Get quiet, close your eyes, and
listen to your heartbeat. It takes a few
minutes until you will be able to hear it.
This exercise will calm your nerves and
center you.
At this point, don’t spend time practicing
your pitch. Have faith that you know it.
Brains tend to shut down under stress.
Spend these few minutes connecting
with yourself and breathing.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
upper Manhattan—working during the days to make ends meet—signed up
for the army, served my time, and applied to Harvard Law School. I wanted to
get into the law since I was six years old. I didn’t have a degree from college.
Harvard took me anyway.
Why?
I wrote them a letter.
One day, one of my professors said that there are these guys starting a firm.
I had a visit with them, and the entire time I met with them, they were telling
me what the risks were of going with a firm that didn’t have a client. The
more they talked, the more I liked them. So I said, What the hell, I’ll take a
chance. They had to scrape together the thirty-six hundred a year, which
was the starting salary. I was their associate. We had a tiny suite of offices on
the top floor of the Lehman Brothers Building on Wall Street. In 1954, I took
over as managing partner, and the firm began to grow by leaps and bounds.
Soon it had one hundred lawyers, and we have continued to grow it since
then.
Build a Compelling Character II (continued) How to Memorize Your Pitch
Memorize your pitch by acting it out
several times.
* Practice your material in chunks.
* Act out your material using strong
emotion.
* Visualize what you are talking about.
* Practice your act as if you are
performing for:
- a group of friends
- stonefaced garden gnomes
- Larry Ellison, Steve Jobs, and Jeff
Bezos
Perform in front of a mirror, or videotape
yourself. You will be surprised at the
results.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
The Problem
When you get a cut or abrasion, a battle rages at the
microbial level between your body’s defenses and bacteria in the
wound. These bacteria begin to form biofilm almost immediately. When
the biofilm is fully formed, the bacteria become resistant to treatment. This
is not a rare problem, and it’s not cheap to deal with. It doesn’t occur in just
the old, the poor, or those in ill health. Treating infected wounds is expensive
and one of the biggest problems we have in this country. 1.7 Million people
get Hospital acquired infections. That’s a $45Billion problem in the US. No
sure that’s real problem? After all healthcare is a third of our $18
trillion economy. 	150,000 amputations annually. 10 million chronic
wounds. 	The problem is growing by 10% a year. Hospital Acquired
Infections are the FOURTH LEADING CAUSE OF DEATH.
Generic Solution
You can solve this problem today. Of course.
Otherwise most of us would die early and painful deaths. The current
solutions are to Remove it [EXAMPLE], Kill it [EXAMPLE] or Cover it
[EXAMPLE].
Deepen the Problem I Emotions Reside In The
Senses
Emotions are experienced and expressed
in writing in five ways:
1. Reactions manifest inside the body --
temperature, heartbeat, muscle reaction,
neural change.
2. Reactions manifest outside the body
-- posture, gesture, facial expression,
tone of voice.
3. Flashes of the past -- moments of
reference, quick bursts of images and
sense impressions.
4. Flashes of the future -- quick bursts
of images and sense impressions of what
will or what may happen.
5. Selection of what is perceived and felt
in the environment -- this ties to NLP,
in that we notice around us those sense
impressions and details that affirm or
reaffirm how we feel, what we believe.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
Our Solution
Eradicate Biofilm, Stop Infection. The Chelexa product
BioLexa eliminates biofilm and cures infection. It does this by
preventing Zinc accumulation. When combined with a broad spectrum
antibiotic, our product prevents the formation of a biofilm so that
antibiotics can kill the bacteria. 	The Product The Chelexa BioLexa
cream is a topical antibiotic ointment with DTPA that prevents biofilm
formation for a variety of wound care infections. It combines two FDA
approved products: 1. kills biofilm. 2. kills bateria Chelexa offers
the world’s only biofilm targeted infection treatment that is more
effective and less expensive than traditional treatments. Costs $7.50
to manufacture Is applicable to over 35 life threatening bacteria
situations saves healthcare providers 95% over the course of an 8 week
treatment.
Deepen the Problem I (continued) Creativity Is Not Just For
Artists
It’s for businesspeople looking for a new
way to close a sale; it’s for engineers
trying to solve a problem; it’s for parents
who want their children to see the
world in more than one way. Creativity
is a habit, and the best creativity is a
result of good work habits. There are
no “natural” geniuses. There is only
hard work. Nobody worked harder than
Mozart: “Nobody devoted so much time
and thought to composition as I. There
is not a famous master whose music I
have not industriously studied through
many times.” My genius is developed
through exercise, through repetition,
through a blend of learning and reflection
that’s both painstaking and rewarding.
Even Mozart needed to get TWENTY-
FOUR youthful symphonies under his belt
before he composed something enduring
with number twenty-five.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
The Problem
If you wanted to describe western North Dakota in just a few words, you might say, Fast Growing Economy. Or
Beautiful Country. Or Nice People. But if you really wanted to capture what is going on, you could simply say, No
Vacancy.
There’s limited housing supply in this fast growing region. Many workers here are living in hotels, mobile homes.
Others live year-round in their RVs.
Why? The growth in the petroleum business is one of the key drivers. But the credit crunch is a culprit too. And
there isn’t enough power, water and emergency infrastructure to support the needed housing growth. Even if
there were infrastructure, communities don’t want outsiders to build “throw-away” housing - so permits are
hard to get.
Generic Solution
A solution to this problem has sprung up. These are so-called man camps, which are dorm-style housing to
accommodate the explosively growing labor force. These man camps are reminiscent of refugee camps, and
neither the local citizenry nor the executives who have no other option particularly care for the solution.
Our Solution
The result of all this? If you owned high qualilty multifamily units in this market, you’d enjoy 100% occupancy,
Deepen the Problem II
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
rising rents, limited competition and high net profits. Across our portfolio, rents are currently $200 to $300 below
market. The market rents in western North Dakota will continue to increase for the foreseeable future. We have a
track record of successful rent lift in the $200-500 range as unit leases expire or units turn over. With the addition
of the corporate furnished apartments. Today, Aalton Real Estate is positioned to benefit from the economic and
population growth in the southern region of the Bakken Formation.
Anchor In Reality I
In fact, we invited our stakeholders to a game of hockey last night. It’s extremely exciting to watch. The arena is
packed. A long red carpet is rolled out on the ice, and the announcer introduces the game’s dignitaries. First comes
the premier of British Columbia, Gordon Campbell. Then, out walks Gordie Howe, one of the legends of the game.
“Ladies and gentlemen,” the announcer booms. “Mr. Hockey!”
For the next sixty minutes, the two teams play aggressive hockey. Vancouver scores first, early in the second period,
on a rebound by Mario Bliznak. Late in the second period, it is Medicine Hat’s turn, as the team’s scoring leader,
Darren Helm, fires a quick shot past
Vancouver’s goalie, Tyson Sexsmith. Vancouver answers in the third period, scoring the game’s deciding goal, and
then, when Medicine Hat pulls its goalie in desperation, Vancouver scores a third time.
Deepen the Problem II (continued)
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
In the aftermath of the game, the players and their families and sports reporters from across the country and our
team cram into the winning team’s locker room. The air
is filled with cigar smoke and the smell of champagne and sweat-soaked hockey gear. On the wall is a handpainted
banner: “Embrace the Struggle.” In the center
of the room the Giants’ coach, Don Hay, stands mistyeyed. “I’m just so proud of these guys,” he says. “Just look
around the locker room. There isn’t one guy who didn’t
buy in wholeheartedly.”
That’s what I mean when I say Canadian hockey is a meritocracy.
Anchor in Reality II
Look, here’s how the Chelexa product works. It’s simple. Nurse admits
a patient. Doctor identifies early signal of infection. Clean wound
with whatever is available. Apply Chelexa solution with standard
field dressing. Monitor. That’s it.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
Connection to the Problem
As some of you know, I served in the Marine Corps, and as a front line medic in Iraq and Afghanistan, I’ve seen
preventable wounds turn into serious illness, amputation and death. My time in the field has connected me deeply to
this part of healthcare, and I’m driving to find better methods to prevent wound infection and save lives. I can’t bring
back the friends and family I’ve lost, but I can make a difference for the next generation.
Human Scale
This isn’t just about 10 million people or a $45B business. 	If you are a hospital administrator, you can get better end
of year reviews by your oversight committee. Hospital Acq. Infection can reduce your rating, and cause you to miss
out on federal funding. 	If you’re a diabetic patient, your chance of complications go down by a third. That can mean
better quality of life. If you’re just coming out of surgery, the Chelexa treatment can save you $50,000 or more in
uncovered insurance if you get a secondary infection.
Future Action Projection
As we go into the future, and take advantage of this market opportunity, you and I will be working together closely.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 11 --

TELL
THE
STORY
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
As the investor, you’re going to get access to research no one else can get. You’re going to see major opportunities as
we work with the government, the military and with hospitals, and we project you’re going to get a 5X return on your
money in the next 24 months.
Dramatic Tension
Based on our achievements, and recognition by both
scientific peer group and investors and customers, we anticipate we’re
going to be fully funded by the end of the month. 	We have the luxury
(and it isn’t always like this) of choosing good partners. As we start
to consider you, might even say evaluate you, I’m interested to hear
about your knowledge in our area, your experience as investors and how
you could contribute to the things we’re working on...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 11 --
